wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the e-commerce platform overview. Explain its purpose as a full-stack Spring Boot + MongoDB application, architecture overview, and relationship to frontend components. Document the implementation details, module organization, and usage patterns. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases. Document the high-level system flow, key features, and business domain coverage. Include diagrams where appropriate to illustrate the overall architecture and component relationships.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Comprehensive Backend Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
This is a full-stack e-commerce platform backend built with Spring Boot 3.2.3 and MongoDB, designed to support high-volume flash sale events and provide comprehensive e-commerce functionality including user authentication, product management, order processing, and real-time promotions.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **High-Performance Scalability**: Optimized for flash sale scenarios with configurable Tomcat thread pools
- **Modern Authentication**: JWT-based authentication with OAuth2 Google integration
- **Comprehensive E-commerce**: Full CRUD operations for products, orders, carts, and user management
- **Real-time Promotions**: Advanced flash sale system with registration and monitoring capabilities
- **Developer Experience**: Comprehensive debugging tools and development utilities

### Target Audience
- E-commerce platform developers
- Backend engineers working on high-traffic applications
- Teams requiring flash sale capability testing infrastructure

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The project follows a layered architecture with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend Clients] --> B[REST API Controllers]
    B --> C[Service Layer]
    C --> D[Repository Layer]
    D --> E[(MongoDB Collections)]
    
    F[Security Layer] --> B
    G[JWT Filter] --> F
    H[CORS Configuration] --> B
    
    I[Background Jobs] --> C
    J[Email Service] --> C
    K[Cloudinary Service] --> C
```

### Design Patterns
- **Layered Architecture**: Clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data access layers
- **Repository Pattern**: MongoDB repositories for data persistence
- **DTO Pattern**: Separate data transfer objects for API responses
- **Factory Pattern**: Service factory for different business operations
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven order processing and notifications

### System Relationships
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant AuthController
    participant AuthService
    participant UserRepository
    participant JwtUtil
    
    Client->>+AuthController: POST /api/auth/login
    AuthController->>+AuthService: authenticate(credentials)
    AuthService->>+UserRepository: findByEmail(email)
    UserRepository-->>-AuthService: User entity
    AuthService->>+JwtUtil: generateToken(user)
    JwtUtil-->>-AuthService: JWT token
    AuthService-->>-AuthController: AuthResponse
    AuthController-->>-Client: 200 OK + token
```

### Data Flow Diagrams
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client Request] --> B[JWT Authentication Filter]
    B --> C[Controller Layer]
    C --> D[Service Layer]
    D --> E[Repository Layer]
    E --> F[(MongoDB Database)]
    F --> E
    E --> D
    D --> C
    C --> G[Response]
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Entry**: `src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/BackendApplication.java` - Spring Boot main class with scheduling enabled
- **Flash Sale Simulator**: `tools/FlashSaleSimulator/src/main/java/simulator/FlashSaleSimulator.java` - High-concurrency testing tool

### Core Modules
- **Authentication Module**: `src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/auth/` - User authentication, OAuth2, JWT handling
- **Product Management**: `src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/product/` - Product CRUD, variants, categories
- **Order Processing**: `src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/order/` - Order lifecycle management
- **Shopping Cart**: `src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/cart/` - Cart operations and persistence
- **Promotions**: `src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/promotion/` - Vouchers and flash sale systems
- **Admin Panel**: `src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/admin/` - Administrative operations

### Configuration Approach
- **Spring Configuration**: `src/backend/src/main/resources/application.properties` - Centralized configuration with environment variables
- **Security Configuration**: `src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/auth/security/SecurityConfig.java` - Comprehensive security setup
- **Build Configuration**: `src/backend/pom.xml` - Maven dependencies and build settings

### External Dependencies
Key dependencies include:
- **Spring Boot 3.2.3**: Core framework with Web, Security, Data MongoDB starters
- **MongoDB**: NoSQL database for flexible schema design
- **JWT**: JSON Web Token for stateless authentication
- **Cloudinary**: Cloud-based image storage and processing
- **OpenCSV**: CSV data import/export functionality
- **Thymeleaf**: Template engine for HTML generation

### Integration Points
- **Google OAuth2**: Third-party authentication integration
- **Email Services**: SMTP integration for password resets and notifications
- **Payment Webhooks**: External payment processor callbacks
- **Cloud Storage**: Image upload and CDN integration

### Component Relationship Diagrams
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Auth Controller] --> B[Auth Service]
    B --> C[User Repository]
    C --> D[User Entity]
    
    E[Product Controller] --> F[Product Service]
    F --> G[Product Repository]
    G --> H[Product Entity]
    
    I[Order Controller] --> J[Order Service]
    J --> K[Order Repository]
    K --> L[Order Entity]
    
    M[Cart Controller] --> N[Cart Service]
    N --> O[Cart Repository]
    O --> P[Cart Entity]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
The platform provides comprehensive e-commerce functionality:

#### Authentication & Authorization
- User registration and login with JWT tokens
- OAuth2 Google authentication integration
- Role-based access control (USER, SELLER, ADMIN)
- Password reset with OTP verification

#### Product Management
- Complete CRUD operations for products and variants
- Category-based filtering and organization
- Image upload and management via Cloudinary
- Real-time inventory tracking

#### Shopping Experience
- Persistent shopping cart with item management
- Order placement and tracking
- Multi-item order processing
- Shipping provider integration

#### Promotional Features
- Flash sale system with time-based campaigns
- Voucher management and redemption
- Dynamic pricing and discount calculations
- Real-time promotional statistics

### Implementation Highlights
- **Flash Sale System**: `src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/promotion/flashsale/` - Advanced concurrent order processing
- **Security Layer**: `src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/auth/security/` - Comprehensive JWT-based authentication
- **Data Persistence**: `src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/**/entity/` - Rich entity models with MongoDB annotations
- **Background Processing**: `src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/**/service/impl/` - Asynchronous task execution

### Feature Architecture Diagrams
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Authenticated: Login Success
    Authenticated --> UserDashboard: Regular User
    Authenticated --> SellerDashboard: Seller Account
    Authenticated --> AdminPanel: Admin Access
    
    UserDashboard --> ProductSearch: Browse Products
    UserDashboard --> ShoppingCart: Add Items
    UserDashboard --> OrderHistory: View Orders
    
    ShoppingCart --> Checkout: Proceed to Payment
    Checkout --> OrderPlaced: Payment Success
    OrderPlaced --> OrderTracking: Processing
    OrderTracking --> OrderDelivered: Delivery Complete
```

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
This backend demonstrates a well-structured, scalable e-commerce platform with modern Spring Boot practices. The architecture effectively separates concerns across multiple modules while maintaining clear data flow and security boundaries.

### Key Characteristics
- **Scalable Design**: Built with high-concurrency flash sale scenarios in mind
- **Modern Security**: JWT-based authentication with OAuth2 integration
- **Flexible Data Model**: MongoDB provides schema flexibility for e-commerce data
- **Comprehensive Testing**: Includes dedicated flash sale simulator for load testing

### Architectural Strengths
- Clean separation of concerns with layered architecture
- Comprehensive security implementation with role-based access control
- Robust error handling and validation throughout the system
- Extensive use of DTOs for API communication
- Well-organized package structure following domain-driven design

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **Microservices Migration**: Consider breaking down large monolithic structure into bounded contexts
- **Caching Strategy**: Implement Redis caching for frequently accessed data
- **API Documentation**: Add OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for better developer experience
- **Monitoring**: Integrate distributed tracing and metrics collection
- **Event Sourcing**: Consider event-driven architecture for complex order workflows

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Implement Caching**: Add Redis caching for product catalogs and user sessions
2. **Add Circuit Breakers**: Implement resilience patterns for external service calls
3. **Enhance Monitoring**: Add APM tools like New Relic or Datadog
4. **API Versioning**: Implement versioning strategy for backward compatibility
5. **Load Testing**: Expand flash sale simulator with more realistic user behavior patterns

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **Performance Optimization**: Profile and optimize critical paths for flash sale events
- **DevOps Enhancement**: Implement CI/CD pipelines with automated testing
- **Feature Expansion**: Add advanced analytics and recommendation engines
- **Mobile Integration**: Develop GraphQL API for mobile client optimization
- **Internationalization**: Add multi-language support for global expansion

**Sources:**
- [BackendApplication.java](src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/BackendApplication.java)
- [pom.xml](src/backend/pom.xml)
- [application.properties](src/backend/src/main/resources/application.properties)
- [AuthController.java](src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/auth/controller/AuthController.java)
- [ProductController.java](src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/product/controller/ProductController.java)
- [OrderController.java](src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/order/controller/OrderController.java)
- [CartController.java](src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/cart/controller/CartController.java)
- [SecurityConfig.java](src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/auth/security/SecurityConfig.java)
- [JwtAuthFilter.java](src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/auth/security/JwtAuthFilter.java)
- [User.java](src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/auth/model/User.java)
- [Cart.java](src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/cart/entity/Cart.java)
- [FlashSaleController.java](src/backend/src/main/java/com/shoppeclone/backend/promotion/flashsale/controller/FlashSaleController.java)
- [FlashSaleSimulator.java](tools/FlashSaleSimulator/src/main/java/simulator/FlashSaleSimulator.java)

</blog>
